my_thoughts: The correction works: not immune (can be destroyed by accident), creation = non-mechanical triad (cannot be formed mechanically), destruction = mechanical (can be destroyed mechanically), analogy (work of art: created by special triad, but can be burnt). The student asked protection question (seeking spiritual immunity), which shows they understand: school = not under law of accident (immunity). They'll likely ask about death next.
accumulated_insight: T3: Student asked protection question ('You spoke of the insecure times we live in. Will the school take precautions to protect itself? I understood you to say that a school is not under the law of accident')—seeking spiritual immunity via asks for protection and seeks rule exemption. They understand: school = not under law of accident (immunity). The structural_analogy mechanism corrects: not immune (can be destroyed by accident), creation = non-mechanical triad (cannot be formed mechanically), destruction = mechanical (can be destroyed mechanically), analogy (work of art: created by special triad, but can be burnt) ('I meant that a school cannot be formed by accident, but it can be destroyed by accident. It cannot be formed mechanically, but it can be destroyed mechanically. Take some work of art—it will not be created purely mechanically, it will be made by some special triad—but it can be burnt like anything else'). Pattern: authenticity check → denial/flexibility rule → control question → qualification/structural model → accident question → correction → [next: death question]. They're grasping: school = not immune (can be destroyed), creation ≠ destruction (correction).
